skill.name: api-documentation
skill.description: Create comprehensive API documentation for developers. Use when documenting REST APIs, GraphQL schemas, or SDK methods. Handles OpenAPI/Swagger, interactive docs, examples, and API reference guides.
skill.summary: Generate professional API documentation with OpenAPI specs, interactive docs, and code examples for REST and GraphQL APIs.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: All 79 static findings are false positives. The skill contains only documentation templates with example URLs and code blocks. No actual shell commands, network exfiltration, or cryptographic vulnerabilities exist. The scanner triggered on markdown code delimiters, placeholder URLs in examples, and JWT authentication mentions in OpenAPI specs.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers create professional API documentation with OpenAPI specs, interactive Swagger UI, code examples, and reference guides for REST and GraphQL APIs.
actual_capabilities: Generate OpenAPI 3.0 specifications with endpoints, parameters, and responses
Create interactive Swagger UI documentation setup
Write code examples in multiple languages (JavaScript, Python, curl)
Document authentication with JWT and OAuth patterns
Structure API docs with guides, references, and changelogs
Define error codes, rate limits, and pagination schemes
limitations: Does not execute or test actual API endpoints
Does not generate SDK client libraries
Requires manual verification of example accuracy
Does not host or deploy documentation

use_cases.title: Internal API Development
use_cases.description: Document internal microservices APIs for frontend-backend team collaboration
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers working with frontend teams

use_cases.title: Public API Release
use_cases.description: Create professional documentation for external developers consuming your API
use_cases.target_user: API product managers and developer relations teams

use_cases.title: SDK Documentation
use_cases.description: Generate comprehensive guides and reference docs for library consumers

prompt_templates.title: Basic OpenAPI Spec
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an OpenAPI 3.0 specification for a REST API endpoint that manages users. Include GET (list), POST (create), GET by ID, PUT (update), DELETE endpoints. Define request/response schemas with proper HTTP status codes.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a starting point for API documentation

prompt_templates.title: Authenticated Endpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Document a protected API endpoint that requires JWT authentication. Include the auth flow, bearer token usage, 401/403 error responses, and example curl commands for authenticated requests.

prompt_templates.title: Error Handling Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive error handling guide for a REST API. Document all common error codes (400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 429, 500), their meanings, example error responses, and recommended client handling approaches.
prompt_templates.scenario: When building robust API error documentation

prompt_templates.title: Interactive Docs Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Swagger UI for an Express.js API. Show how to integrate swagger-ui-express, load an OpenAPI YAML file, configure custom CSS, and mount the docs at /api-docs.

output_examples.input: Create OpenAPI spec for user management API
output_examples.output: A complete OpenAPI 3.0 specification with /users endpoint, pagination, authentication, and response schemas
Step-by-step guide: 1. Login endpoint returns JWT, 2. Include Bearer token in Authorization header, 3. Token refresh mechanism, 4. Example curl commands
Organized docs with: Overview, Authentication, Endpoints grouped by resource, Error Codes, Rate Limiting, Changelog

best_practices: Provide working code examples in multiple languages (JavaScript, Python, curl)
Always document both success and error response cases with examples
Keep documentation synchronized with API changes using changelogs
anti_patterns: Using real API keys or passwords in example code
Leaving vague descriptions like 'returns data' without specifics
Missing error case documentation for failure scenarios

faq.question: What OpenAPI version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports OpenAPI 3.0.0 specification with examples for both YAML and JSON formats.

faq.question: Can I generate documentation for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: Yes, you can document GraphQL schemas using the same documentation patterns. Include schema definitions, query/mutation examples, and type definitions.

faq.question: How do I set up interactive Swagger UI?
faq.answer: Use swagger-ui-express for Node.js or swagger-ui-react for React. Load your OpenAPI YAML file and mount at a route like /api-docs.

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported?
faq.answer: Common methods include JWT bearer tokens, API keys, and OAuth 2.0. The skill provides templates for documenting auth flows.

faq.question: Can this skill test my actual API endpoints?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates documentation content. Use tools like Postman or curl to test actual endpoints separately.

faq.question: How do I document rate limiting?
faq.answer: Include rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining), document limits per endpoint, and explain retry-after behavior.
